Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember the supply chain activities we have SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS The temporal profile needs pairs that are eventually following each other right Not just direct consecutive ones but also ones separated by multiple steps like SS to CA OP to PK etc

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First lets list out the direct pairs first those are obvious because they are directly connected in the process flow

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: SS to OP Usually once you select a supplier you place the order pretty quickly right Like within a day Wait no actually maybe a few hours

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait no wait lets think SS is the activity of choosing the supplier so once thats completed the next step is placing the order

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets make the direct pairs first then the non direct ones which have longer times because they span multiple steps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First direct pairs consecutive steps in the process flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: OP to RC Order placement to receive components thats supplier lead time which for high tech components can be variable

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RC to QI Usually once components arrive inspection starts same day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: WS to DT Thats the time the product sits in the warehouse before shipping

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: DT to AS after sales support happens when the customer uses it so the time between DT and AS would vary a lot

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now we need non direct pairs too because the problem says pairs that eventually follow each other not just directly connected

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS to RC Thats SS to OP plus OP to RC So average would be the sum

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Since the main variability is from lead time OP to RC so stdev is about 3 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RC to PT Thats RC to QI to CA to PT Lets add up the averages

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the main variability comes from CA to PT so overall stdev is around half a day

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS to DT Supplier selection to distribution Thats SS all the way to DT

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the main variability is OP to RC and WS to DT

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the time to after sales support is mostly how long the customer uses the product before having an issue which varies a lot

Name: Validation - PE
Text: all pairs are where the first activity comes before the second in the process flow right Because you cannot have OP before SS or RC before OP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: maybe adjust WS to DT average to 5 days instead of 7 to make the total SS to DT about 20 days

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait lets make sure the values are correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now non direct pairs SS to RC OP to QI PT to WS PK to DT SS to QI OP to CA QI to PK CA to WS SS to CA OP to PK RC to WS PT to AS SS to PT OP to WS RC to DT QI to AS SS to AS

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check if all these pairs are valid that is the first activity comes before the second in the process flow All of these are valid

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: the pairs cover all levels of separation direct 1 step 2 steps up to the full process which meets the requirement of complexity

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the direct pairs have lower stdevs relative to their averages except for the lead time and warehouse dwell time which are correctly variable
